commit.message:
feat: v0.3.0 — status, check cmd, profiles, 5 new checks, unit tests

zbx status:
- New command: connection info, Zabbix API version, template/host counts
- Added version_str property to ZabbixClient (reads version set during login)

zbx check list / info / install:
- List all bundled checks in configs/checks/ with item/trigger/agent summary
- Info: detailed template fields, triggers with severity colour, agent block
- Install: zbx apply + zbx agent deploy --from-check in one step

Multi-environment profiles:
- zbx --profile <name> or ZBX_PROFILE env var
- Reads zbx.profiles.yaml (gitignored); zbx.profiles.yaml.example added
- ConfigLoader.load_settings() respects profile > ZBX_PROFILE > env_file > env

5 new bundled checks (check.yaml + script + agent block):
- mysql: connections, query throughput, InnoDB buffer pool, slow queries
- rabbitmq: messages ready/unacked, connections, consumers, memory
- haproxy: frontend/backend/server status, sessions, errors (stdlib only)
- elasticsearch: cluster status, shards, JVM heap, doc count
- kubernetes-node: CPU/memory, pod density, node conditions via Kubelet API

Unit tests (66 new, 88 total — all passing in 36s):
- tests/test_models.py: 51 tests — validation, enums, defaults, HostMacro format
- tests/test_diff_engine.py: 15 tests — ADD/MODIFY/UNCHANGED/EXTRA logic (no live Zabbix)

Also:
- HostMacro.macro field validator enforces {$…} or {#…} format
- .gitignore: zbx.profiles.yaml added

v0.4.0 (#19)

* Add Syzygy tablebase support and Zobrist hashing implementation (#8)

* Add Syzygy tablebase support and Zobrist hashing implementation

- Implemented Syzygy endgame tablebase support in `tablebase.rs`, enabling probing of WDL and DTZ results for various piece configurations.
- Added functionality to detect available tablebase files, determine piece configurations, and gracefully handle missing files.
- Introduced Zobrist hashing in `zobrist.rs` for efficient board position representation, supporting transposition table lookups and three-fold repetition detection.
- Included tests for both modules to ensure correctness and reliability of the implementations.

* Update CI workflows to include 'development' branch for push and pull_request events

* Add initial documentation setup with VitePress and logo SVG (#9)

- Created package.json for CheckAI documentation with scripts for development, build, and preview.
- Added logo.svg for branding in the documentation.

* Refactor search depth handling, enhance game storage, and improve Zobrist hashing

- Clamp max search depth to prevent out-of-bounds access in the search function.
- Add Clone derive for GameStorage struct to facilitate easier cloning of game archives.
- Update Zobrist hashing to use lowercase 'p' for black pawns in filename generation.
- Modify piece Zobrist index to accommodate both uppercase and lowercase pawn representations.
- Introduce en passant legality check to ensure accurate hashing only when a capture is possible.
- Add unit test to verify that the hash remains consistent when no en passant capture is available.

* Refactor Zobrist hashing logic for clarity and conciseness

- Improved readability of comments in the Zobrist hashing implementation.
- Simplified conditional checks in `has_ep_capture_candidate` and `hash_position` functions by using `if let` syntax for better clarity.

* Refactor test for Polyglot keys to use iterators for improved readability

Fix rendering pipeline: force 8-bit surfaces, deferred object rendering

Major fixes:
- Force 8-bit palettized pixel format in TDrawArea::SetSize CreateSurface.
  cnc-ddraw emulates 8-bit mode using 16-bit surfaces internally, causing
  Pitch=2*Width and garbled panel/HUD rendering. Explicitly requesting
  DDSD_PIXELFORMAT with DDPF_PALETTEINDEXED8 ensures proper 8-bit surfaces.
- Implement deferred object rendering in view_function_terrain. Objects were
  drawn mid-tile-loop and overwritten by subsequent overlapping terrain tiles.
  Now collect object draw params during tile loop, execute all draws in a
  post-tile-loop pass with CurSpanList=SpanList (full-surface).
- Add Credits Screen auto-skip in autoplay harness.
- Add diagnostic logging for sprite rendering pipeline.

* fix(claude-adapter): suppress task tool events from root stream

Task tools (task, launch_agent, agent) are visually represented by the
sub-agent tree via stream.agent.start/complete events, not as standalone
tool parts. Suppress stream.tool.start and stream.tool.complete bus
events for task tools to prevent them from appearing at the root of the
sub-agent tree. Internal metadata tracking and subagent correlation are
preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(claude-adapter): route sub-agent text to agent tree via agentId

When sub-agent text arrives in processStreamChunk with a
parentToolCallId that maps to a known sub-agent (via
toolUseIdToSubagentId), publish it as stream.text.delta with agentId
instead of stream.tool.partial_result. This routes the text into the
agent's inline parts in the sub-agent tree rather than to a
(now-suppressed) root tool part.

Fixes sub-agent output appearing above the tree and spinner getting
stuck after the previous commit suppressed task tool bus events.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(stream-pipeline): enforce agent done-state ordering contract for Claude sub-agent streaming

Introduce an agent ordering contract to guarantee that done-state projection
(agent status → completed) always precedes any post-complete delta rendering
for Claude sub-agents, eliminating the race condition where deltas could
render before the terminal status was reflected in the UI.

- Add AgentOrderingContract utility (agent-ordering-contract.ts) with
  idempotent done-state projection, sequence tracking, and state pruning
- Extend StreamPipeline and StreamPipelineConsumer to track completion
  sequences and defer post-complete deltas until done projection is observed
- Update chat.tsx to wire ordering contract into the event loop: register
  completion sequences, flush deferred deltas on done projection, and emit
  observability counters/histograms for projection latency and rendered timing
- Update ParallelAgentsTree and part display components to call back into
  done-rendered markers for end-to-end ordering diagnostics
- Add shouldDeferPostCompleteDeltaUntilDoneProjection and
  queueAgentTerminalBeforeDeferredDeltas exported helpers for testability
- Add stream-lifecycle-run-guard tests, mention-ordering integration and e2e
  tests, and ordering contract unit tests to cover the full contract surface

consensus: warn on producer signature mismatch during sync (don't reject)

During historical sync a fresh node has stale public keys in its
masternode registry (loaded from disk before the chain was rebuilt).
Collateral-UTXO checks prevent announcements from updating those keys
until the node has synced enough UTXOs, creating a dead-lock where
every synced block fails signature verification.

The hash chain still guarantees block integrity. Changing the hard
error to a logged warning lets a syncing node catch up; once it
reaches the tip the registry is refreshed via live announcements and
real-time blocks are verified correctly.

* feat: auto-refresh stale MA player metadata on monitor connect

When MaMonitor establishes a WS connection to MA, it now fetches players/all
and compares each bridge player's device_info.product_name and manufacturer
against the current VERSION and hostname. If stale (e.g. after a version
upgrade) and the player is not actively playing, it sends a reconnect command
to the subprocess. The subprocess disconnects from MA and reconnects with a
fresh client_hello, updating product_name and manufacturer in MA's player DB.

- services/ma_monitor.py: _refresh_stale_player_metadata() called once per
  connect session, after auth and before event subscription
- services/daemon_process.py: handle {cmd: reconnect} → daemon._client.disconnect()
- sendspin_client.py: expose send_reconnect() public async method

* fix: pause_all play uses MA syncgroup API for grouped players

Previously pause_all with action='play' sent {cmd: play} directly to each
subprocess, bypassing MA group sync and potentially breaking grouped playback.

Now:
- pause: one Sendspin session-group command per unique group_id (unchanged)
- play: for each player mapped to an MA syncgroup → ma_group_play() once per
  unique MA syncgroup ID. Falls back to Sendspin session-group command if MA
  not configured or player has no syncgroup. Solo players always use direct cmd.

commit.message:
fix(plugin-ts): prevent barrel from exporting non-existent runtime consts for empty enum schemas (#2637)

* Initial plan

* chore: add temporary test files (will be cleaned up)

Co-authored-by: stijnvanhulle <5904681+stijnvanhulle@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent barrel from exporting non-existing runtime consts for empty enum schemas

When enum schemas have no items (empty `enum: []` or all-null `enum: [null]`)
and `enumType: 'asConst'` or `asPascalConst` is configured, the barrel file
would try to export a runtime const that doesn't exist in the TypeScript file.

Fix: In `factory.createEnumDeclaration`, when `enums.length === 0`, return
`[undefined, neverTypeAlias]` instead of `[variableStatement, indexedAccessTypeAlias]`.
This prevents the const from being registered as indexable (via the
`{nameNode && ...}` guard in Type.tsx) and generates a semantically correct
`type XKey = never` alias instead of a broken const reference.

Fixes: #2564

Co-authored-by: stijnvanhulle <5904681+stijnvanhulle@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: unify env file format detection into validated_extension

`validated_extension` in `seal.rs` is now the single source of truth
for detecting file format from a filename. It recognises:
  - `.env`, `.env.<suffix>` (hidden dotenv files)
  - `<name>.env` (e.g. `development.env`) — previously missed

`stem_extension` in `kv.rs` is simplified to a thin adapter: strips
the `.age` suffix and delegates to `validated_extension`, so store-file
mode (get/set) uses the identical rules as sealed-file mode (seal/unseal
/edit/get/set).

Bugs fixed:
  - `.env.age` previously returned a Usage error (no extension on stem)
  - `.env.staging.age` previously returned format "staging" instead of "env"
  - `development.env` (sealed) was rejected as unsupported extension

Specs updated: req-112 AC1, req-116 AC3 and AC9 to document the full
set of supported env filename patterns.

Replace native alert()/confirm() with tpen-alert/tpen-confirm modal dialogs (#496)

* Initial plan

* Replace alert() and confirm() with tpen-alert/tpen-confirm modal dialogs

Co-authored-by: cubap <1119165+cubap@users.noreply.github.com>

* Stop page bump when scroll bar is showing

* Fix race condition, accessibility, stacking, and button text in confirm dialogs

Co-authored-by: cubap <1119165+cubap@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add confirmAction helper and update confirms

Introduce confirmAction in api/events.js to centralize confirm dialog handling with unique confirmId to avoid race conditions. Revamp ConfirmContainer to queue dialogs, handle keyboard navigation, manage focus, and dispatch confirm events with confirmId. Improve AlertContainer with keyboard handling and focus management. Replace many ad-hoc TPEN.eventDispatcher one/off/dispatch confirm patterns across components (annotorious-annotator, plain, decline-project, leave-project, manage-layers, manage-pages, manifest-import, navigation-manager, quicktype-manager, roles-handler, manage-project interface) to use confirmAction, simplifying code and reducing listener collisions. Also adjust redirect behavior after alerts in manage-project to wait for acknowledgement.

* Update events.js

* Replace modal divs with <dialog> and improve modals

Convert various modal/overlay containers to use the native <dialog> element and its showModal()/close() API. Update Alert and Confirm components to call showModal when showing and to toggle classes safely. Replace section/div overlays with dialog elements in AlertContainer, ConfirmContainer, ProjectTools, QuickTypeEditorDialog, and UpdateMetadata; add backdrop styles, sizing, and [open] display rules. Add cancel and backdrop click handlers to close dialogs (and prevent default cancel behavior). Ensure screen-locking sections are closed/cleaned up after dialogs dismiss (including retry logic in AlertContainer and delayed close in ConfirmContainer). Misc CSS tweaks to support full-viewport dialogs and backdrop behavior.

* Add modalHost utility for less repetition and coupling

Introduce utilities/modalHost.js (openModalHost, closeModalHost, closeModalHostWhenEmpty) to centralize dialog host open/close logic and timing retries. Replace scattered showModal/close logic in Alert.js and Confirm.js to use openModalHost; update AlertContainer.js and ConfirmContainer.js to use closeModalHostWhenEmpty for coordinated closing after animations. Add addCustomAlert and a shared keyboard handler in AlertContainer, and simplify transcription code to call addCustomAlert directly. Files changed: components/gui/alert/Alert.js, components/gui/alert/AlertContainer.js, components/gui/confirm/Confirm.js, components/gui/confirm/ConfirmContainer.js, interfaces/transcription/index.js, utilities/modalHost.js.

* deduping CSS

* TPEN colors first

* Queue alerts and refine modal/confirm UX

Introduce alert queuing and improve modal/confirm behavior and styling. Alerts are now queued (only one shown at a time) with addAlert/addCustomAlert pushing entries to a queue; show/dismiss logic uses a #showCurrent/dismissCurrent flow, native dialog cancel events for Escape, and Enter handling for activation. Confirm dialogs now use role="alertdialog", default focus is set to the negative/cancel button, keyboard navigation is improved (Tab/Shift+Tab cycling, Enter activation through shadow DOM), and positive/negative handlers validate confirmId against the active dialog. Both containers now append their singleton to document.body, and cancel handlers are removed when dialogs are dismissed to avoid leaks. Shared modal CSS was updated (transparent backdrop, color variables, selector changes to alertdialog, button layout and responsive tweaks). Also fixed an import in line-parser to use the utilities confirmAction path.

* Remove dead handler, the condition is always false.  Let <button> do its native thing.
